visa_types.name: Culture
visa_types.documents_required:
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing on the territory of Ukraine who wish to apply for a Culture visa category following documents are required:
- Visa Application form
- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.
- Please note, visa application form will not be accepted if it is filled in paper form. Application form has to be filled only in the system of Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                                    of Croatia and printed. The link to the online application form can be found in the section "Download Form".
- Filled out either in Ukrainian language with English transliteration or in English or Croatian languages. The online form needs to be printed after filling and signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. You don't need to
                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Proof of  activity (at least one must be submitted).
- For employed applicants: Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, date of employment, and the duration of vacation.
- For private entrepreneurs: Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate.
- For students: Student card.
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- Bank Statement on the account status with obligatory specifying of a remaining balance. Also the check from the ATM with the copy of a bank card can be accepted. Please, pay attention that the certificate of cash flow will not be accepted as proof of
                                                                                    solvency.
- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
- Notarized sponsorship letter and a proof of income of the sponsor.
- Hotel or other reservation
- Hotel reservation or voucher from travel agency needs to contain: personal data of each applicant, date of intended stay, reservation number, hotel address, contact phone
                                                                                    number.
- For rented private apartments: confirmation of the reservation from the owner (period of stay, names of each applicant, passport numbers) and copy of decision of the Croatian
                                                                                    authorities on the legal status of renting business called RJEŠENJE.
- Invitation
- From host organization - competent authorities to participate in scientific, cultural or artistic activities held in Croatia, containing the following information of the invited person (full name, gender, nationality, date of birth, purpose of travel
                                                                                    and / or character events, stay and number of entries).
- If the inviting organization covers the costs of travel, meals and accommodation during the stay in Croatia, this fact must be clearly stated in the letter.
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the applicant’s personal situation:
- For foreign citizens:
- Valid residence permit (copy of both sides). Has to be valid for at least 3 months beyond the departure from the territory of Croatia. In case the holder has applied for the renewal of the proof of legal residence he will be requested to submit such document.
- For minors (age under 18):
- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parents’ internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling with one of the parents.
- Copy of the valid Croatian / Schengen visa of the parent travelling together with the child, if the parent submits his/her application not at the same time.
IMPORTANT: To obtain the Croatian visa for a minor child, endorsed in the passport of a parent who has a valid Schengen visa, this parent has to obtain a visa to Croatia
                                                                        for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed. 
ATTENTION:

                                                                        The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation. In all cases the visa fees are non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission process.
visa_types.visa_fees:
[hrv > ukr > en > Table:Culture > Visa Fees 1]
Service fees: is payable in local currency Hryvnya (UAH) by all applicants.

                                                                        Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnya (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate defined by NBU. Fees are inclusive of 20% VAT.
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Application Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv and Odessa.
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.
visa_types.processing_time:
Normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is approximately 5 working days. For the nationals of those countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing
                                                                            time is within 15 working days. 
In case the documents are not submitted as per the requirements or due to other factors, the processing time may take up to 30 calendar days. 
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your
                                                                            application. 
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this website carefully.
                                                                            The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.
visa_types.forms:
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors only in online form. The VAF has to be filled out in Ukrainian language with English transliteration
                                                                            or in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Please note that you have to fill in the Croatian Visa Application online in capital letters, to do it please click here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/).
                                                                        
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form Filling service for the convenience of the applicants at the Centre. The service is purely
                                                                            optional and the applicants may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH75 (all taxes included).  Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling
                                                                            service. 
According to the Embassy's requirements, an applicant who applies for a multiple-entry visa has to provide completed and signed Declaration on Travel medical insurance. To download
                                                                            this form, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf). Please, note that fields "Holder of short-stay visa" and "Stamp"
                                                                            should be leaved blank.
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each applicant applying
                                                                            for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed
                                                                            personally by the applicant even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency. 
Personal Data Information  Agreement (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3mblf3soma4RPoMQNqUcCV/96265fe8782bf017cec14660c5bc3ad0/pdia-croatia.pdf)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This type of visa is applicable for those who wish to travel to Croatia in order to attend cultural events.
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into the
                                                                        country.
                                                                    
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- Representative of a scientific organization with power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the organization (even for children). Group considered a number of customers more than
                                                                            5 people.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D). 
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- Representative of a scientific organization with power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the organization (even for children). Group considered a number of customers more than
                                                                            5 people.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D). 
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

- White background.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

- Customer himself
- For minors only parents or legal guardians (for property owner visa category).
- Representative of a scientific organization with power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the organization (even for children). Group considered a number of customers more than
                                                                            5 people.

                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D).

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa is applicable for those who plan to participate in conferences, forums, exhibitions and seminars. It is also applicable to real estate owners in Croatia. Customer will
                                                                        have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into the
                                                                        country. 
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- For minors only parents or legal guardians (for property owner visa category).
- Representative of a scientific organization with power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the organization (even for children). Group considered a number of customers more than
                                                                            5 people.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D). 
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > ukr > en > Table:Other: Participation in Conferences, Forums, Exhibtions, Seminars; Real Estate > Visa Fees 1]
Service fees: is payable in local currency Hryvnya (UAH) by all applicants.

                                                                        Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnya (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate defined by NBU. Fees are inclusive of 20% VAT.
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Application Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv and Odessa.
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

## Documents Required
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing on the territory of Ukraine who wish to apply for Participation in Conferences, Forums, Exhibitions and Seminars visa category following documents are required:
- Visa Application form
- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.
- Please note, visa application form will not be accepted if it is filled in paper form. Application form has to be filled only in the system of Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                                    of Croatia and printed. The link to the online application form can be found in the section "Download Form".
- Filled out either in Ukrainian language with English transliteration or in English or Croatian languages. The online form needs to be printed after filling and signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. You don't need to
                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the
Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Proof of  activity (at least one must be submitted).
- For employed applicants: Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, date of employment, and the duration of vacation.
- For private entrepreneurs: Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate.
- For students: Student card.
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- Bank Statement on the account status with obligatory specifying of a remaining balance. Also the check from the ATM with the copy of a bank card can be accepted. Please, pay attention that the certificate of cash flow will not be accepted as proof of
                                                                                    solvency.
- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
- Notarized sponsorship letter and a proof of income of the sponsor.
The financial document of the applicant has to prove the amount of at least EUR 70 per person per day. If the hotel is fully prepaid or the sponsorship provided or the inviting
                                                                                person/organisation covers the costs of travel, in this case the applicant should prove the amount at least 30 euro per day per person.
- Invitation
- for members of the professions participating in international exhibitions, conferences, symposia, seminars or other similar events a written request from the host organization
                                                                                    confirming that the person concerned is participating in the event;
- in case of visit of fares/conferences - proof of registration or a copy of a ticket;
- for pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purposes of study or educational training, including in the framework of
                                                                                    exchange programs – a written request or a certificate of enrolment from the host university, college or school or student cards or certificates of the courses to be attended.
Invitation should contain: full name, date of birth, sex, citizenship, passport number, time and purpose of the journey and a number of entries for the invited person.
                                                                                    Names of the minor children in case they accompany the invited person. Invitation should also contain full name and address of the person who invites;
- The use of Real Estate (private or via private firm)
- The use of private real estate – extract from the land registry (ownership certificate) [izvadak iz zemljišne knjige (vlasnički list)], date of issue should not exceed 3 months.
- The use of real estate via private company – extract from the Commercial Court (the register) [izvadak iz trgovačkog suda (sudski registar)] and extract from the land registry
                                                                                    (ownership certificate) [izvadak iz zemljišne knjige (vlasnički list)], date of issue should not exceed 3 months.
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the applicant’s personal situation:
- For foreign citizens:
- Valid residence permit (copy of both sides). Has to be valid for at least 3 months beyond the departure from the territory of Croatia. In case the holder has applied for the renewal of the proof of legal residence he will be requested to submit such document.
- For minors (age under 18):
- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parents’ internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling with one of the parents.
- Copy of the valid Croatian / Schengen visa of the parent travelling together with the child, if the parent submits his/her application not at the same time.
IMPORTANT: To obtain the Croatian visa for a minor child, endorsed in the passport of a parent who has a valid Schengen visa, this parent has to obtain a visa to Croatia
                                                                        for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed. 
ATTENTION:

                                                                        The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation. In all cases the visa fees are non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is approximately 5 working days. For the nationals of those countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time
                                                                        is within 15 working days. 
In case the documents are not submitted as per the requirements or due to other factors, the processing time may take up to 30 calendar days. 
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this website carefully.
                                                                        The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors only in online form. The VAF has to be filled out in Ukrainian language with English transliteration
                                                                        or in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Please note that you have to fill in the Croatian Visa Application online in capital letters, to do it please click here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/).
                                                                    
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form Filling service for the convenience of the applicants at the Centre. The service is purely
                                                                        optional and the applicants may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH75 (all taxes included).  Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.
According to the Embassy's requirements, an applicant who applies for a multiple-entry visa has to provide completed and signed Declaration on Travel medical insurance. To download this
                                                                        form, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf). Please, note that fields "Holder of short-stay visa" and "Stamp"
                                                                        should be leaved blank.
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each applicant applying
                                                                        for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed
                                                                        personally by the applicant even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency. 
Personal Data Information  Agreement (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3mblf3soma4RPoMQNqUcCV/96265fe8782bf017cec14660c5bc3ad0/pdia-croatia.pdf)

- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.

                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas .
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Proof of activity.
- For employed applicants: Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, date of employment, and the duration of vacation.
- For private entrepreneurs: Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- Bank Statement on the account status with obligatory specifying of a remaining balance. Also the check from the ATM with the copy of a bank card can be accepted. Please, pay
                                                                                    attention that the certificate of cash flow will not be accepted as proof of solvency.
- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
- Notarized sponsorship letter and a proof of income of the sponsor.
- Hotel or other reservation (at least one must be  submitted).
- Hotel reservation or voucher from travel agency needs to contain: personal data of each applicant, date of intended stay, reservation number, hotel address, contact phone number.
- For rented private apartment: confirmation of the reservation from the owner (period of stay, names of each applicant, passport numbers) and copy of decision of the Croatian authorities on the legal status of renting business called RJEŠENJE.
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.

- If an applicant is not a citizen of Ukraine he needs to submit a Letter of Guarantee (JAMSTVENO PISMO PRAVNA OSOBA (http://www.mvep.hr/files/file/obrasci/JamstvenoPismoPravnaOsoba.pdf)) with the guarantor's signature on the letter of guarantee and document providing its liquidity (BON1/BON2) or certificate
                                                                                    of the Commercial Court that no liquidation proceedings have been initiated.

                                                                                    of the applicants, date of birth, gender, citizenship, number of the international passport, time and purpose of journey, number of entries and name of minor children
                                                                                    accompanying the invited person if there are. Also the invitation should contain the data about inviting legal person, company or organization: full name, address, name
                                                                                    and position of the person who signs the request.
- If the inviting person is a legal person or company or an office or a branch of such legal person or company established in the territory of a Member State - the registration
                                                                                    number is required by the national law of the Member State concerned.

- For foreign citizens:

                                                                        for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed.

                                                                        The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation. ## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses

                                                                        for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed

## Overview
This type of visa caters to people who tend to visit Croatia for a service visit. These customers are the members of official delegations who plan to participate in meetings, consultations,
                                                                        negotiations or exchange programs. Customer will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than 90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into the
                                                                        country.
                                                                    
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- Representative of a company for its group of colleagues with power of attorney issued on the letterhead of the company (that means colleague for colleague). Group considered a number
                                                                            of customers more than 5 people.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D).
                                                                    
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > ukr > en > Table:Official Delegation Visit > Visa Fees 1]
Service fees: is payable in local currency Hryvnya (UAH) by all applicants.

                                                                        Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnya (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate defined by NBU. Fees are inclusive of 20% VAT.
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Application Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv and Odessa.
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

## Documents Required
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing on the territory of Ukraine who wish to apply for an Official Delegation visa category following documents are required:
- Visa Application form
- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.
- Please note, visa application form will not be accepted if it is filled in paper form. Application form has to be filled only in the system of Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                                    of Croatia and printed. The link to the online application form can be found in the section "Download Form".
- Filled out either in Ukrainian language with English transliteration or in English or Croatian languages. The form online needs to be printed after filling and signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. You don't need to
                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Hotel or other reservation (at least one must be presented).
- Hotel reservation or voucher from travel agency needs to contain: personal data of each applicant, date of intended stay, reservation number, hotel address, contact phone
                                                                                    number.
- For rented private apartments: confirmation of the reservation from the owner (period of stay, names of each applicant, passport numbers) and copy of decision of the Croatian
                                                                                    authorities on the legal status of renting business called RJEŠENJE.
- Invitation from host company: a letter issued by an Ukrainian authority confirming that the applicant is a member of its delegation travelling to participate at the aforementioned
                                                                                events, accompanied by a copy of the official invitation. 
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the applicant’s personal situation:
- For foreign citizens:
- Valid residence permit (copy of both sides). Has to be valid for at least 3 months beyond the departure from the territory of Croatia. In case the holder has applied for the
                                                                                    renewal of the proof of legal residence he will be requested to submit such document.
IMPORTANT: To obtain the Croatian visa for a minor child, endorsed in the passport of a parent who has a valid Schengen visa, this parent has to obtain a visa to Croatia
                                                                        for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed. 
ATTENTION:

                                                                        The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation. In all cases the visa fees are non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is approximately 5 working days. For the nationals of those countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time
                                                                        is within 15 working days. 
In case the documents are not submitted as per the requirements or due to other factors, the processing time may take up to 30 calendar days.
                                                                    
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.
                                                                    
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this website carefully.
                                                                        The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors only in online form. The VAF has to be filled out in Ukrainian language with English transliteration
                                                                        or in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. 
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Please note that you have to fill in the Croatian Visa Application online in capital letters, to do it please click here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/).
                                                                    
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form Filling service for the convenience of the applicants at the Centre. The service is purely
                                                                        optional and the applicants may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH75 (all taxes included).  Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.
                                                                    
According to the Embassy's requirements, an applicant who applies for a multiple-entry visa has to provide completed and signed Declaration on Travel medical insurance. To download this
                                                                        form, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf). Please, note that fields "Holder of short-stay visa" and "Stamp"
                                                                        should be leaved blank.
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each applicant applying
                                                                        for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed
                                                                        personally by the applicant even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency.
Personal Data

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This type of visa is applicable to a third-country national who has been granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit, and requires a short-term visa to enter the Republic of Croatia.
Long-term visa (visa D) is issued for one (1) or multiple (MULT) entries, with the period of validity no longer than six months and for maximum 30-days' stay.
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- For minors (only parents or legal guardians).
- By accredited travel agencies.
The list of accredited travel agencies (http://ua.mfa.hr/files/file/dmku/ua/agencije.pdf)
.
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.
Ordinance on Visas (Official Gazette, No. 109/21), entering into force 1 November 2021, additionally clarifies the procedure and conditions of the issuance of long-term visas to third-country nationals in Croatian Embassies/Consulates.
The long-term visa is an approval to stay in the territory of the Republic of Croatia up to 30 days if a third-country national has been granted temporary stay for the purposes referred to in Article 57 of the Aliens Act:
- family reunification,
- secondary school education,
- university studies,
- research,
- humanitarian grounds,
- life partnership,
- work;
- work of a posted worker;
- stay of long-term residents in another EEA Member State,
- stay of digital nomads,
- other purposes
and is required to hold a short-term visa to enter the Republic of Croatia.
Visa type D substitutes the procedure of short-term (type C) visa applications of third-country nationals who have been granted temporary stay or issued stay and work permit by the competent Police Directorate/Police Administration.

-

- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.

- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.

- Proof of kinship (marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.)
- Copy of passport of Croatian national or copy of residence permit in Croatia
- Invitation should contain: full name, date of birth, gender, citizenship, passport number, time and purpose of the journey and a number of entries for the invited person. Names of the minor children in case they accompany the invited person. Invitation should also contain full name and address of the person who invites.
- If applicant is a friend or a distant relative of a Croatian or Ukrainian national who is legally residing in Croatia:
- Letter of Guarantee (JAMSTVENO PISMO FIZIČKE OSOBE (http://www.mvep.hr/files/file/obrasci/JamstvenoPismoFizickaOsoba.pdf)) in original. The guarantor's signature on the letter of guarantee shall be certified by a public notary.

- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parents’ internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling with one of the parents.
- Copy of the valid Croatian / Schengen visa of the parent travelling together with the child, if the parent submits his/her application not at the same time.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa is applicable for those who intend to visit Croatia for tourism.
                                                                    
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than  90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into
                                                                        the country. 
                                                                    
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- For minors only parents or legal guardians.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D).
                                                                    
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > ukr > en > Table:Visit to family or friends > Visa Fees 1]
Service fees: is payable in local currency Hryvnya (UAH) by all applicants.

                                                                        Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnya (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate defined by NBU. Fees are inclusive of 20% VAT. 
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Application Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv and Odessa.
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

## Documents Required
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing on the territory of Ukraine who wish to apply for a Tourist visa category following documents are required:
- Visa Application form
- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.
- Please note, visa application form will not be accepted if it is filled in paper form. Application form has to be filled only in the system of Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                                    of Croatia and printed. The link to the online application form can be found in the section "Download Form".
- Filled out either in Ukrainian language with English transliteration or in English or Croatian languages. The online form needs to be printed after filling and signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. You don't need to
                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Proof of  activity (at least one must be submitted).
- For employed applicants: Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, date of employment and the duration of vacation.
- For private entrepreneurs: Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate.
- For students: Student card.

- 
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- Bank Statement on the account status with obligatory specifying of a remaining balance. Also the check from the ATM with the copy of a bank card can be accepted. Please, pay attention that the certificate of cash flow will not be accepted as proof of solvency.
- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
- Notarized sponsorship letter and a proof of income of the sponsor.
The financial document of the applicant has to prove the amount of at least EUR 70 per person per day. If the hotel is fully prepaid or the sponsorship provided or the inviting person/organisation covers the costs of travel, in this case the applicant should prove the amount at least 30 euro per day per person.
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.
- Invitation
- If applicant is a close relative: spouses, children (including adopted), parents (including custodians), grandparents and grandchildren of citizens of Croatia or Ukraine legally residing on the territory of the Republic of Croatia.
- Proof of kinship (marriage certificate, birth certificate, etc.)
- Copy of passport of Croatian national or copy of residence permit in Croatia
- Invitation should contain: full name, date of birth, gender, citizenship, passport number, time and purpose of the journey and a number of entries for the invited person. Names of the minor children in case they accompany the invited person. Invitation should also contain full name and address of the person who invites.
- If applicant is a friend or a distant relative of a Croatian or Ukrainian national who is legally residing in Croatia:
- Letter of Guarantee (JAMSTVENO PISMO FIZIČKE OSOBE (http://www.mvep.hr/files/file/obrasci/JamstvenoPismoFizickaOsoba.pdf)) in original. The guarantor's signature on the letter of guarantee shall be certified by a public notary.
- Financial document(s) of inviting person – work or bank or pensioner’s statement. Requirements for these documents are mentioned at point 6. Can be submitted in original or in copy.
- If an applicant is a family member of EU/EEA or Swiss citizens (according to Directive 2004/38/EC) - Proof of kinship and a copy of the residence permit in Croatia.
Necessary documents depending on the applicant’s personal situation:
- For foreign citizens:
- Valid residence permit (copy of both sides). Has to be valid for at least 3 months beyond the departure from the territory of Croatia. In case the holder has applied for the renewal of the proof of legal residence he will be requested to submit such document.
- For minors (age under 18):
- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parents’ internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling with one of the parents.
- Copy of the valid Croatian / Schengen visa of the parent travelling together with the child, if the parent submits his/her application not at the same time.
IMPORTANT: To obtain the Croatian visa for a minor child, endorsed in the passport of a parent who has a valid Schengen visa, this parent has to obtain a visa to Croatia for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed.
ATTENTION:
The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation. In all cases the visa fees are non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is approximately 5 working days. For the nationals of those countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing
                                                                            time is within 15 working days. 
In case the documents are not submitted as per the requirements or due to other factors, the processing time may take up to 30 calendar days.
                                                                        
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your
                                                                            application. 
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this website carefully.
                                                                            The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors only in online form. The VAF has to be filled out in Ukrainian language with English transliteration
                                                                            or in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
Please note that you have to fill in the Croatian Visa Application online in capital letters, to do it please click here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/).
                                                                        
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form Filling service for the convenience of the applicants at the Centre. The service is purely
                                                                            optional and the applicants may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH75 (all taxes included).
                                                                             Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.
                                                                        
According to the Embassy's requirements, an applicant who applies for a multiple-entry visa has to provide completed and signed Declaration on Travel medical insurance. To download
                                                                            this form, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf). Please, note that fields "Holder of short-stay visa" and "Stamp"
                                                                            should be leaved blank.
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each applicant applying
                                                                            for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed
                                                                            personally by the applicant even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency.
Personal Data 
                                                                                                  Information Agreement (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3mblf3soma4RPoMQNqUcCV/96265fe8782bf017cec14660c5bc3ad0/pdia-croatia.pdf)

## Overview
This type of visa is applicable for those who transit through the territory of Croatia to a final country of their destination
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than  90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into
                                                                        the country. 
                                                                    
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- For minors only parents or legal guardians.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D).
                                                                    
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

                                                                                    upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph

                                                                        for both applicants - for the child and for the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This type of visa is applicable for those who intend to visit Croatia for tourism.
                                                                    
It allows the citizens of Ukraine to travel to Croatia and stay for a limited period of time of not more than  90 days in a 6 months period from the date of the first entrance into
                                                                        the country. 
                                                                    
Visa application can be submitted by:
- Customer himself
- For minors (only parents or legal guardians).
- By accredited travel agencies. The list of accredited travel agencies (http://ua.mfa.hr/files/file/dmku/ua/agencije.pdf)
.
Please, pay your attention that those customers who have a valid Schengen visa with at least two entries can stay in Croatia for up to 90 days in a six months period without opening a
                                                                        Croatian visa. The same is true for holders of a valid residence permit issued by one of the Schengen member states and the holders of a valid long term Schengen visa (type D).
                                                                    
To read more about fees, the list of required documents, visa application processing time, as well as to download the necessary forms, please click on horizontal links above.

## Visa Fees

[hrv > ukr > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Service fees: is payable in local currency Hryvnya (UAH) by all applicants.

                                                                            Please note that all the fees are payable in Hryvnya (UAH) at the Cashier counter inside the Visa Application Centre according to the weekly exchange rate defined by NBU. Fees are inclusive of 20% VAT.
Credit card payments are available at the Visa Application Centres in Kiev, Lviv, Kharkiv and Odessa.
Currency exchange is available at the Visa Application Centres in Odessa and Kharkiv.
The purchase of medical insurance policy service is available at the Visa Centres in Kiev, Kharkiv and Odessa.

## Documents Required
For citizens of Ukraine and permanent residents legally residing on the territory of Ukraine who wish to apply for a Tourist visa category following documents are required:
- Visa Application form
- Completed for each applicant including children. It has to be filled out online.
- Please note, visa application form will not be accepted if it is filled in paper form. Application form has to be filled only in the system of Ministry of Foreign Affairs
                                                                                    of Croatia and printed. The link to the online application form can be found in the section "Download Form".
- Filled out either in Ukrainian language with English transliteration or in English or Croatian languages. The online form needs to be printed after filling and signed personally
                                                                                    by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page. You don't need to upload your photo to the system.
- For minors, the application has to be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
- One photograph 
- 35 x 45 mm in color.
- White background.
- Not older than 6 months.
- Passport or other travel document.
- International passport.
- Copy of the first page of international passport with personal information.
- Copies of the previous Schengen, US or other visas.
- Passport should be valid for at least three months after the date of visa expiration.
- Passport must be issued within the last 10 years and should contain at least two blank pages.
- Extended passports will not be accepted.
- Medical travel insurance.
- Coverage of 30.000 Euro.
- Covering Croatia and valid for all the European member states for the whole duration of journey.
- For multiple entries visa, insurance must be valid for the first trip and applicant needs to fill out the Declaration on travel medical insurance (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf) for subsequent visits.
- Hotel or other reservation (at least one must be submitted).
- Hotel reservation or voucher from travel agency needs to contain: personal data of each applicant, date of intended stay, reservation number, hotel address, contact phone number.
- For private apartment rentals: confirmation of the reservation from the owner (period of stay, names of each applicant, passport numbers) and copy of
                                                                                    decision of the Croatian authorities on the legal status of renting business called RJEŠENJE.
- For nautical tourism: written confirmation from cruising agency (name of the ship, name of the port/s in Croatia, date and time of the arrival/departure);
                                                                                    registration papers of ownership; rent-a-boat invoice).
- Proof of activity (at least one must be submitted).
- For employed applicants: Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, date of employment and the duration of vacation.
- For private entrepreneurs: Certificate of registration of private entrepreneur.
- For pensioners: Pension certificate.
- For students: Student card.
- Financial documents (at least one must be submitted).
- Work statement on the company letterhead indicating position, salary of an applicant gained within the previous period of 6 months.
- Bank Statement on the account status with obligatory specifying of a remaining balance. Also the check from the ATM with the copy of a bank card can be accepted. Please, pay attention that the certificate of cash flow will not be accepted as proof of
                                                                                    solvency.
- Travel checks, under the name of the applicant, duly signed, presented in original and copy
- Pensioner statement from the Pension Fund of Ukraine indicating the amount of pension gained for the last 6 months.
- University certificate that proves the accrual of scholarships for the last 6 months.
- Notarized sponsorship letter and a proof of income of the sponsor.
- The financial document of the applicant has to prove the amount of at least EUR 70 per person per day. If the hotel is fully prepaid or the sponsorship provided, in this case the applicant should prove the amount at least 30 euro per day per person. 
- Proof of intended transport (at least one must be presented).
- Tickets reservation.
- Copy of the technical passport of the vehicle, green card and a copy of driver’s license for those who will travel by own vehicle.
Necessary documents depending on the applicant’s personal situation:
- For foreign citizens:
- Valid residence permit (copy of both sides). Has to be valid for at least 3 months beyond the departure from the territory of Croatia.
- In case the holder has applied for the renewal of the proof of legal residence he will be requested to submit such document.
- For minors (age under 18):
- Birth certificate.
- Copies of the first pages of parents’ internal passports.
- Notarized consent from both of the parents in case of a minor travelling alone or a notarized consent from the parent who is not travelling in case a minor travelling with
                                                                                    one of the parents.
- Copy of the valid Croatian / Schengen visa of the parent travelling together with the child, if the parent submits his/her application not at the same time.
IMPORTANT: To obtain the Croatian visa for a minor child, endorsed in the passport of a parent who has a valid Schengen visa, this parent has to obtain a visa to Croatia
                                                                        for both applicants - the child and the parent in whose passport the child is endorsed. 
ATTENTION:
The Embassy of Croatia has the right to ask for any other additional documents necessary, may call the applicant for an interview, and keeps the right for refusal without any explanation.
                                                                        In all cases the visa fees are non-refundable.
Please note that you may purchase medical insurance policy at the Visa Application Centre as well as order form filling by Visa Application Centre manager during submission process.

## Photo Specifications
A Photograph submitted with the application for a visa should comply with the following specifications:
- Must be an up-to-date photograph (preferably maximum 6 months old)
- Must be a color photograph printed on normal photographic paper
- Should be passport sized 3,5 x 4,5 cm
- Close up to your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70-80% of the photograph
- Full face taken straight forward 30 - 36 mm from point of the chin to head of hair
- Taken against a light background
- Without sunglasses
- Without hat or other head covering unless you wear this for religious reasons
- Spectacles should not have toned or reflecting glasses
- The frame of the spectacles may not cover the eyes
- The mouth must be closed

## Processing Time
Normal processing time for Ukrainian nationals is approximately 5 working days. For the nationals of those countries who do not have a visa facilitation agreement, the processing time
                                                                        is within 15 working days. 
In case the documents are not submitted as per the requirements or due to other factors, the processing time may take up to 30 calendar days.
                                                                    
Please note that you may be required to come for a personal interview with the visa officer at the Embassy of the Republic of Croatia in Kyiv prior to a decision being made on your application.
                                                                    
Embassy also reserves the right to ask for additional supporting documents during the visa application process. Applicants are requested to read the information on this website carefully.
                                                                        The guidelines listed here are intended to help you to prepare your documents as accurately as possible. This will reduce the chances of your application being incomplete or delayed.

## Download Forms
Visa application form has to be completed for all the applicants including minors only in online form. The VAF has to be filled out in Ukrainian language with English transliteration
                                                                        or in English or Croatian languages. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian.
                                                                    
Please note that you have to fill in the Croatian Visa Application online in capital letters, to do it please click here (https://crovisa.mvep.hr/).
                                                                        Tourists should mention NA in the section "Gurantor / iniviting person information". But the information about the hotel has to be filled obligatorily in the section "Information
                                                                        on the residence in the Republic of Croatia".
Please note that the Visa application Centre is introducing the Form Filling service for the convenience of the applicants at the Centre. The service is purely
                                                                        optional and the applicants may avail this service at a nominal price of UAH75 (all taxes included).
                                                                         Please contact the VAC staff to know more about the Form Filling service.

                                                                    
According to the Embassy's requirements, an applicant who applies for a multiple-entry visa has to provide completed and signed Declaration on Travel medical insurance. To download this
                                                                        form, please, click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3FUOMQdiGacXpLBglTd6wb/05d46e583d5594d0bec40d91699e9ac2/izjavaputnozdrosiguranje_150613.pdf). Please, note that fields "Holder of short-stay visa" and "Stamp"
                                                                        should be leaved blank.
According to the Law of Ukraine About personal information, Personal Data Information Protection Agreement has been introduced and is required to be signed by each applicant applying
                                                                        for a visa through the Visa Centres. For minors this agreement should be signed by one of the parents or a legal guardian. We pay you attention that such agreement has to be signed
                                                                        personally by the applicant even if submitting documents through a representative or accredited travel agency.
                                                                    
Personal Data 
                                                                                                                Information Agreement (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3mblf3soma4RPoMQNqUcCV/96265fe8782bf017cec14660c5bc3ad0/pdia-croatia.pdf)
